title: Steam Trap Station Assembly
description: Assemble the steam trap station components in correct order from the steam equipment outlet to the condensate return main.
explanation: A steam trap station requires a dirt leg to protect the trap from debris, isolation valves on both sides for maintenance, and a check valve downstream to prevent reverse flow from the condensate return. This arrangement follows ASHRAE and ASME B31.1 best practices.

title: PRV Station Assembly
description: Assemble the pressure reducing valve station components from the high-pressure supply to the low-pressure distribution.

parts.name: Safety relief valve (set above reduced pressure)

explanation: A PRV station must have a strainer upstream to protect the valve internals, a pressure gauge downstream for monitoring, and a safety relief valve to protect against PRV failure. The relief valve is critical — if the PRV fails open, full supply pressure reaches the low-pressure side per ASME B31.1.

title: Pump Piping Assembly
description: Assemble the piping components at a base-mounted centrifugal pump from the suction side through to the discharge piping.

parts.name: Concentric reducer at pump suction (flat on top)
Flexible connector at pump suction flange
Flexible connector at pump discharge flange
Check valve and discharge isolation valve

explanation: Pump piping requires a specific arrangement: suction-side gate valve (never throttled), eccentric/concentric reducer (flat side up to prevent air pockets), flexible connectors on both flanges for vibration isolation, and check valve before discharge isolation valve per ASHRAE and ASME B31.9.

title: Boiler Near Piping Assembly
description: Assemble the boiler near piping from the hot water boiler outlet through the supply header components.

parts.name: Boiler supply outlet with isolation valve
Air separator (installed at highest temperature point)

explanation: Boiler near piping places the air separator at the point of lowest gas solubility (hottest point, immediately after the boiler). The expansion tank connects at the pump suction — this is the point of no pressure change, ensuring the tank maintains proper system pressurization per ASME B31.9 and ASHRAE.

title: Chiller Evaporator Piping
description: Assemble the chilled water piping at a chiller evaporator from the building return to the building supply.
explanation: Chiller evaporator piping requires a strainer upstream to protect heat exchanger tubes, a flow switch to prevent operation without water flow (which causes ice formation), and balancing/isolation valves for commissioning and maintenance per manufacturer requirements and ASHRAE guidelines.

title: Expansion Tank Piping
description: Assemble the diaphragm expansion tank piping connection from the system piping to the tank.

parts.name: Tee connection at pump suction (point of no pressure change)

explanation: The expansion tank must connect at the pump suction — the point of no pressure change. This ensures the pump adds pressure to the system (not subtracts it), keeping the system above atmospheric pressure everywhere and preventing air ingestion per ASHRAE and ASME B31.9.

title: Air Separator Assembly
description: Assemble the air separator and related components in a hydronic heating system piping loop.

parts.name: Hot water supply from boiler (highest temperature point)
Automatic air vent on top of separator

explanation: The air separator is installed at the point of lowest air solubility — the hottest point in the system, right after the boiler. The automatic vent releases separated air, and the expansion tank connects nearby at the same low-pressure point per ASHRAE Handbook and ASME B31.9.

title: Backflow Preventer Assembly
description: Assemble the reduced pressure zone (RPZ) backflow preventer station on a boiler makeup water line.

parts.name: RPZ backflow preventer with test ports
Air gap drain from relief port to floor drain

explanation: RPZ backflow preventers protect potable water from contamination by boiler chemicals. The strainer protects the RPZ check valves, isolation valves allow annual testing, and the relief port must drain to an air gap (not direct connection) per plumbing code and ASSE 1013.

title: Medical Gas Zone Valve Box
description: Assemble the components inside a medical gas zone valve box for an oxygen zone in the correct order from the main supply to the zone distribution.

parts.name: Zone inlet connection from main riser
Zone outlet to branch piping serving outlets

explanation: Medical gas zone valve boxes per NFPA 99 must contain a labeled zone shutoff valve, pressure gauge, and pressure switch connected to the area alarm system. The valve allows emergency shutoff of the zone while the alarm system notifies clinical staff of any pressure changes. This is a life-safety installation.

title: Glycol Fill Station
description: Assemble the glycol fill station components for charging a closed-loop hydronic system.
explanation: Glycol must be pre-mixed to the correct concentration before filling — never dump straight glycol into a system. The fill pump provides pressure to push fluid into the pressurized system, and the strainer prevents contaminants from entering. Always verify concentration with a refractometer after filling per ASHRAE guidelines.

title: Strainer and Control Valve Train
description: Assemble the control valve train from the supply header to a heating coil, including all required accessories.

parts.name: Heating coil supply and return connections

explanation: The control valve train sequence is critical: strainer before control valve (protects valve seat), control valve on supply side (for accurate control), balancing valve on return (for commissioning). This arrangement follows ASHRAE 90.1 and standard hydronic piping practice per ASME B31.9.

title: Heat Exchanger Piping Assembly
description: Assemble the primary and secondary side piping at a plate-and-frame heat exchanger for a building heating system.

parts.name: Primary supply isolation valve and strainer
Primary side of heat exchanger (hot in, cooled out)
Primary return isolation valve with P/T ports
Secondary circulator pump with check valve
Secondary side of heat exchanger (cold in, heated out)
Secondary supply to building with isolation and balancing valves

explanation: Heat exchanger piping connects primary (district/boiler) and secondary (building) loops. Counter-flow arrangement maximizes heat transfer efficiency. The strainer protects primary exchanger plates, P/T ports allow performance monitoring, and the secondary pump circulates building water independently per ASHRAE and ASME B31.9.
